input_parameters.name: fromCalculations
description: A document (IData object) containing the values to combine with toCalculations.

children.name: count
description: Optional. Number of entries in the result set for the window. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: max
description: Optional. Maximum value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: min
description: Optional. Minimum value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: sum
description: Optional. Sum of the values for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: first
description: Optional. The first value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied.

children.name: last
description: Optional. The last value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied.

input_parameters.name: toCalculations
description: A document (IData object) containing the calculations to combine with fromCalculations.

children.name: count
description: Optional. Number of entries in the result set for the window. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: max
description: Optional. Maximum value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: min
description: Optional. Minimum value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: sum
description: Optional. Sum of the values for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied. The supplied value must be a number that is represented as a String.

children.name: first
description: Optional. The first value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied.

children.name: last
description: Optional. The last value for the parameter in the window to which the CALCULATE step was applied.

output_parameters.name: toCalculations
description: The toCalculations document (IData object) combined with the values in fromCalculations.

children.name: average
description: This value is returned only if both sum and count were included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations, and count is not zero.

children.name: count
description: This value is returned only if count was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

children.name: max
description: This value is returned only if max was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

children.name: min
description: This value is returned only if min was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

children.name: sum
description: This value is returned only if sum was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

children.name: first
description: This value is returned only if first was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

children.name: last
description: This value is returned only if last was included in fromCalculations and/or toCalculations.

usage_notes: The CALCULATE step in event processing applies to a specific parameter. The pub.streamProcessing:combineCalculations service combines calculations performed on the same parameter in multiple windows. The service could combine two windows or add the current window with running calculations.
For example, if the fromCalculations specifies:
The result of pub.streamProcessing:combineCalculations has toCalculations with:
If you next execute pub.streamProcessing:combineCalculations to combine the running toCalculations with the following fromCalculations from the current window:

A common use case for the pub.streamProcessing:combineCalculations service is to maintain running calculations that span multiple windows. For example, if you had an event trigger that produced calculations for each minute of the day, you could use this service to cache the results for the entire day.
This can be done in the event trigger target service using the following steps:
1. Fetch the running calculations from the cache.
2. Combine the current window's calculations with the running calculations using the pub.streamProcessing:combineCalculations service.
3. Write the new calculations back to the cache.
